larity		 Explanation
Rhythm		Explanation
Key		Start of TuneExplanation
All titles in use for this tune (plus comments about the tune)
JigDG Major Kesh Jig, The / Kesh Jig / The Kesh / The Keash Jig / Keash Jig / Keash / The Kincora Jig / The Kincora / The Castle Jig / Little Boy Ted in the Hay / The Comely Maid / Kerrigan's / The Mountaineer's March (for 3rd figure of The West Kerry Set on JRSLCB 2; 2nd in set The Kesh Jig on BowBros) (also in A or C or D) (compare The Mountaineers' March and The Spring Well included here from O'Neill books, compare mainly the 2nd part with the 2nd part of Kesh #7119; compare 1st part with 1st part of Lark on the Strand #1093, with 1st part of Sean Coughlan's Kesh #2554, with 1st part of Terry Lane's #6564, and with 2nd part of #3663)
JigDA Major Sean Coghlan's Kesh [Sean Coughlan's Kesh] (3rd in set Tomaisín a' Rí on BgCn; for 5th figure of Taille Mhara Jig Set on MISD 7) (compare 1st part with 1st part of Kesh Jig #1022)
JigDG Major Jackson's Jig / Jackson's / Port Mhic Eoin / Jacksons / Hogan's Favorite / The Humours of Kesh / Humours of Kesh / Pléaráca na Céise / The Merry Bonnaveen / The Tempest / Barron's / The Gudgeon of Maurice's Car (1st in set Jackson's on MC; 1st in set Jigs on 4MD 1; 1st in set Up Sligo on CLnn 1) (compare 1st part with 1st part of Kerfunken Jig #796 and 1st part of O'Neill's The Gudgeon of Maurice's Car, included here) (composed by Walker Jackson, possibly (often misspelled as Walter Jackson))
JigDD Major Kesh, The (compare mainly the 2nd part with the 2nd part of Kesh #1022)
JigDG Major Terry Lane's (compare 1st part with 1st part of Kesh #1022 and Lark on the Strand #1093)
JigDA Mixolydian [untitled] (2nd in set Haley's Favourite on JKm) (1st part same as 1st part of Sweet Biddy Daly #815; compare 2nd part with 1st part of Kesh Jig #1022)
JigDE Minor Coleman's Cross / Road to Kesh / Paddy Clancy's / Kimmel's No. 2 (3rd in set La queue de l'hirondelle on Barde 1; 3rd in set The Wandering Minstrel Medley on MLIIA) (also parts reversed, also as slide?)
JigDD Major Strike the Gay Harp / Tarraing ar an Chláirseach Mheidhreach / Meidhir na Cruite / Strike the Young Harp / The Night Dance / The Piper's Welcome (mislabeled as Willie Coleman's on ST BST reissue; 2nd in set Jigs on MCny 2; 3rd in set The Kesh Jig on BowBros; 2nd in set Hawthorn Hedge on BrgGapMM) (also in G or C) (compare The Piper's Welcome included here in book 1001) (composed by Walker Jackson ca. 1774)
ReelA Dorian Eel in the Sink, The / An Eascann sa Doirteal / Eel in the Sink / The Irishman's Blackthorn / Maide Draighin an Éireannaigh / The Blackthorn / The Hills of Kesh / Cnocáin na Céise (3rd in set Jenny Picking Cockles on Frwhlr) (also doubled, also in B, also in Mixolydian) (compare Irish Blackthorn #7771 and John Naughton's #5670 and Old Grey Gander #6364: and #2792; 2nd part same as 2nd part of Sheeren's Reel #6192 and compare 2nd part of Swallow's Tail #1855)
JigDD Major Queen of the Fair, The / Queen of the Fair / Ríon an Aonaigh / Banríon an Aonaigh (1st in set Queen of the Fair on StWing 1; 2nd in set Jigs on the Green on KCB 5; 1st in set The Kesh Jig on BowBros) (also without 3rd part) (compare, quite distantly, Moloney's Wife #317 and compare 1st part with 1st part of Paddy Doory's Fancy #4740) (composed by John McFadden)
JigDG Major Lark on the Strand, The / The Lark in the Strand / Lark in the Strand / An Fhuiseog ar an Trá / The Primrose Vale / The Primrose Glen / Gleanntán na Samhaircíní / Wicky Sears (also in D or A) (compare 1st part with 1st part of Kesh Jig #1022 and with 1st part of Terry Lane's #6564)
